growth_orientation_rationale: The tax concession and increased funding are designed to enhance R&D activities, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation.
status_evidence: The tax concession and funding measures have been reaffirmed and are currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The introduction of a significant tax concession and increased public funding represents a major reform in R&D policy.
description: The Government has introduced a 150 per cent tax concession for R&D to stimulate research and development activities, alongside increased spending on public infrastructure projects and tax incentives to encourage investment, including accelerated depreciation allowances for plant and equipment.
source_quote: The budget included increased spending on public infrastructure projects and labour market programmes. Taxation measures included the introduction of new royalty withholding tax arrangements, the extension of the Fringe Benefits Tax to include employer provided parking and the maintenance of the 150 per cent write off for eligible research and development expenditure.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for health services is expected to improve health outcomes in Aboriginal communities, thereby enhancing overall productivity and social welfare.
status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and is currently in effect.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a major structural change in funding for Aboriginal health services.
description: The FY 1994/95 Commonwealth Budget boosted funding for Aboriginal health services by almost A$ 500 million, extending the Aboriginal Health Strategy until FY 1998/99, with a significant portion allocated to improving health services and basic infrastructure in Aboriginal communities.
source_quote: The FY 1994/95 Commonwealth Budget boosted funding for Aboriginal health services by almost A$ 500 million, to be spent over the next five years.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing human capital through education and training is vital for fostering innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented and are currently in operation.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes aimed at improving the skills and training of the workforce.
description: The Australian government intends to enhance the supply of skilled workers through various reforms, including the removal of obstacles to work-based vocational education and training (VET), the introduction of the National Literacy and Numeracy Strategy, and the implementation of 'user choice' in apprenticeship and traineeship training.
source_quote: It funded pilot projects for 'user choice' in apprenticeship and traineeship training which is to be the basis of the provision of training for all apprenticeships and traineeships from the beginning of 1998.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing productivity through improved industrial relations is expected to lead to higher economic output.
status_evidence: The reforms have been in effect since their introduction in 1996.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant structural change in industrial relations aimed at improving productivity.
description: The reforms of industrial relations arrangements in Australia, initiated in 1987 and intensified through the Workplace Relations Act 1996, aimed to enhance productivity by promoting decentralised bargaining and reducing regulatory barriers for small and medium-sized firms.
source_quote: The reforms introduced a large measure of regulatory flexibility in the industrial relations system especially by facilitating and encouraging the setting of wages and employment conditions at the enterprise level.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for R&D is expected to enhance research capabilities and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The funding programme has been successfully introduced and is currently in operation.
importance_rationale: The significant increase in funding represents a structural change in public investment in R&D.
description: The Australian government has significantly increased funding for research in higher education institutions, tripling it from A$ 480 million in 1983/84 to an estimated A$ 1.5 billion in 1995/96. Additionally, a new R&D funding programme called START has been introduced, allocating A$ 340 million over four years in competitive grants to support research and development.
source_quote: Government funding for research in higher education institutions tripled from A$ 480 million in 1983/84 to an estimated A$ 1.5 billion in 1995/96.

growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to enhance the availability of capital for innovative firms, thereby fostering entrepreneurship and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented and are operational.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes aimed at improving the startup ecosystem and access to funding.
description: The Innovation Investment Fund programme (IIF) was established to create early-stage venture capital funds that focus on commercialising R&D, with the Australian Government contributing A$130 million to leverage private sector investment. Additionally, the Pooled Development Funds (PDFs) programme was initiated to increase the supply of patient equity capital to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) through tax incentives and support. Furthermore, proposals under the Corporate Law Economic Reform Program were released to facilitate capital raising by SMEs, allowing companies to raise up to A$2 million each year from up to 20 persons without issuing a prospectus.
source_quote: The Government has also recently released proposals under the Corporate Law Economic Reform Program aimed at facilitating capital raising by small and medium enterprises.

growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying compliance processes, the initiative aims to lower the administrative burden on businesses, potentially fostering entrepreneurship and innovation.
status_evidence: The government intends to establish a single registration process by mid-1998.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards reducing compliance costs for businesses, which can enhance their operational efficiency and encourage new enterprise creation.
description: The Australian government intends to establish a single registration process for various taxation and regulatory bodies to reduce duplication of reporting for businesses.
source_quote: By mid-1998 the government intends to establish a single registration process for the Taxation Office, Securities Commission, Bureau of Statistics and the Insurance and Superannuation Commission.

growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging later retirement can help maintain a skilled workforce and support economic productivity.
status_evidence: The scheme has been introduced and is operational.
importance_rationale: The reform is significant in its aim to influence workforce participation but is not as large-scale as the R&D funding measures.
description: The Australian government introduced a scheme providing a bonus for individuals who defer taking up the age pension and remain in full-time employment, aimed at encouraging later retirement.
source_quote: The government introduced in July 1998 a scheme which provides a bonus to persons who remain in full-time employment and defer taking up the age pension.

growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the number of skilled graduates in critical fields, the reform aims to enhance the innovation capacity of the workforce, which can lead to higher productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: Funding is to be provided for additional targeted university places each year.
importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in human capital aimed at fostering innovation, particularly in key technological and scientific fields.
description: Australia's innovation system is supported by funding for additional targeted university places, prioritizing programs in ICT, mathematics, and science to enhance foundational skills necessary for successful innovation.
source_quote: Funding is also to be provided for 2000 additional targeted university places each year with priority on programmes in the areas of ICT, mathematics and science.

growth_orientation_rationale: The package is designed to stimulate R&D investment and innovation, which are critical for economic growth and competitiveness.
status_evidence: The measures are set to be implemented over the next five years.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in innovation infrastructure and R&D, aiming to close the gap with OECD averages and enhance Australia's competitive position.
description: The Australian government launched a A$3 billion innovation package, Backing Australia’s Ability, aimed at enhancing the country's capacity for research, encouraging the commercialization of ideas, and developing skills over the next five years.
source_quote: Recognising the importance of innovation as a driver of sustainable economic growth and increased competitiveness, the government launched in January 2001 a A$3 billion innovation package, Backing Australia’s Ability.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increases public investment in R&D, which can enhance innovation capacity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The programme was launched to address weaknesses in R&D and innovation.
importance_rationale: This programme represents a significant commitment to enhancing Australia's R&D infrastructure and funding, which is crucial for long-term economic growth and innovation.
description: The Backing Australia’s Ability programme, launched in January 2001, aims to provide a comprehensive and integrated package to boost infrastructure and research funding, addressing barriers to innovation in Australia.
source_quote: The Backing Australia’s Ability programme, launched in January 2001, is designed to provide a comprehensive and integrated package boosting infrastructure and research funding.

growth_orientation_rationale: The programme aims to reduce greenhouse gas emissions through innovative projects, thereby supporting environmental sustainability and potentially enhancing economic efficiency.
status_evidence: The programme was operational with funding allocated for greenhouse gas abatement projects.
importance_rationale: The GGAP represents a significant investment in environmental innovation, targeting multiple projects to reduce emissions, but it is not a comprehensive overhaul of existing policies.
description: The Greenhouse Gas Abatement Programme (GGAP) was established to provide A$400 million over four years for competitive subsidies aimed at greenhouse gas abatement projects, including renewable energy and energy efficiency initiatives.
source_quote: As part of the Measures for a Better Environment, announced at the end of 1999... A$400 million of public money is to be provided over the next four years for large-scale greenhouse gas emission reductions.

growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative promotes the transition to renewable energy, which can lead to long-term sustainability and reduced reliance on fossil fuels.
status_evidence: The allocation of funds for the conversion of generators was part of the broader environmental measures.
importance_rationale: This funding represents a targeted effort to enhance renewable energy use in specific regions, contributing to environmental goals but not fundamentally altering the energy policy landscape.
description: A$264 million was allocated for the conversion of diesel-fuelled electricity generators to renewable energy technology in remote areas, aiming to enhance the use of renewable energy sources.
source_quote: Another measure included in that same package is the allocation of up to A$264 million over the next four years to the conversion of diesel-fuelled electricity generators to renewable energy technology in remote, off-grid areas.

growth_orientation_rationale: The tax rebate incentivizes small businesses to invest in R&D, which can lead to innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The scheme has been operational since its introduction in 2001.
importance_rationale: The R&D Offset scheme is a major reform aimed at boosting R&D investment among small businesses.
description: The Government introduced the R&D Offset scheme as the principal support mechanism to increase R&D performed by small businesses in Australia, providing a tax rebate for R&D expenditure.
source_quote: The R&D Offset scheme is available to all companies with an annual turnover of less than A$5million.

growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying federal awards is expected to improve regulatory flexibility, potentially leading to increased productivity.
status_evidence: The legislation has been enacted and is currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The reform is significant in enhancing regulatory flexibility but does not represent a major overhaul.
description: The Australian Government has introduced legislation to simplify federal awards, aiming to define and specify allowable award matters more clearly, which is expected to enhance regulatory flexibility in workplace arrangements.
source_quote: The Workplace Relations Amendment (Award Simplification) Bill 2002, if passed, will more clearly define and specify allowable award matters and incidental matters.

growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing skill shortages and enhancing workforce capabilities, this initiative is expected to improve productivity and support economic growth.
status_evidence: The strategy has been executed and is actively addressing skill shortages.
importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant effort to improve skills in the workforce, although it is not as transformative as the higher education funding reform.
description: The Australian Government launched the National Skills Shortages Strategy to address skill shortages in key industries, incorporating new approaches to apprenticeships and encouraging regional collaboration.
source_quote: In 2004, the government launched the National Skills Shortages Strategy to tackle skill shortages in key industries throughout the country.

growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to improve water access and market efficiency, which can enhance productivity in various sectors.
status_evidence: The NWI has been fully implemented as part of Australia's water reform agenda.
importance_rationale: The NWI represents a significant structural change in water management policy.
description: The National Water Initiative (NWI) was agreed upon to enhance water reform in Australia, focusing on improving the security of water access entitlements and encouraging the expansion of water markets and trading.
source_quote: The NWI aims to improve the security of water access entitlements, ensure ecosystems’ health and encourage the expansion of water markets and trading.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding and improved alignment with economic needs are expected to enhance the quality of higher education, thereby supporting long-term economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been put into effect and are currently operational.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes in the funding of higher education, impacting the entire sector.
description: The Australian government launched a review of the higher education sector, resulting in a A$1½ billion increase in support to universities over four years and the introduction of a new Commonwealth Grant Scheme (CGS) to replace traditional block operating grants. This reform aims to align funding with the economy's needs and improve the quality of higher education.
source_quote: Important initiatives included: a A$1½ billion increase in support to universities over four years; changes in commonwealth funding for higher institutions; greater flexibility for universities in setting course fees.

growth_orientation_rationale: The focus on health, education, and training is expected to enhance the skills and capabilities of the workforce, driving innovation and productivity.
status_evidence: The agenda has been actively pursued and implemented by COAG since its agreement.
importance_rationale: The National Reform Agenda represents a comprehensive approach to reforming human capital development in Australia.
description: In February 2006, COAG agreed to collaborate on the implementation of a new National Reform Agenda that includes a human capital agenda focusing on health, education, and training, alongside traditional areas of competition and regulatory reform.
source_quote: In February 2006, COAG agreed to collaborate on the implementation of a new National Reform Agenda to enhance productivity and labour force participation.

growth_orientation_rationale: These funding initiatives are designed to bolster educational infrastructure, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The funds have been established and are operational as part of the government's education strategy.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant commitment to enhancing educational funding and infrastructure.
description: The establishment of the Education Investment Fund in May 2008 aims to enhance funding for higher education, while the Australian government has created three new funds to prioritize investment in infrastructure, education, and health care, with a total initial endowment of approximately AUD40 billion.
source_quote: The establishment in May 2008 of the Education Investment Fund (which absorbs and extends the AUD5 billion Higher Education Endowment Fund) could help in this respect.

growth_orientation_rationale: This plan aims to enhance water resource management, which is vital for sustainable development and economic growth.
status_evidence: The plan has been implemented as part of the government's strategy for water resource management.
importance_rationale: The plan represents a significant investment in water management infrastructure.
description: The Water for the Future plan, with a budget of AUD12.9 billion over ten years, aims to improve the governance of the Murray-Darling Basin and develop an integrated management system for water resources, including a national information system for water data.
source_quote: The new national plan, Water for the Future, aims to improve the governance of the MDB and to develop an integrated management of the Basin.

growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to improve digital infrastructure, which is critical for innovation and economic development.
status_evidence: The plans for the fibre optic network have been publicly announced and are in the planning stages.
importance_rationale: The establishment of a national broadband network represents a significant investment in infrastructure.
description: The Australian government plans to establish a private/public partnership to build a fibre optic network, contributing up to AUD4.7 billion to ensure 98% of the population has access to high-speed broadband connections, while also considering a functional separation between infrastructure management and marketing activities to encourage the construction of this network.
source_quote: The government is planning to establish a private/public partnership to build a FTTN fibre optic network, with a government contribution of up to AUD4.7 billion.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance infrastructure, which is critical for economic growth and productivity.
status_evidence: The reforms have been put into action as part of the government's infrastructure strategy.
importance_rationale: The establishment of the Building Australia Fund and the National Broadband Network represent significant structural changes in infrastructure policy.
description: The Australian government has established the Building Australia Fund to finance infrastructure projects, transitioning to the Regional Infrastructure Fund funded by a new federal tax on mining profits. Additionally, Infrastructure Australia was created to advise on infrastructure policy and conduct audits for public and private investment needs. A major reform project has also been initiated to develop a National Broadband Network (NBN), replacing the existing copper telecommunications network with a fibre optic network managed by a public enterprise to ensure fair access for private operators.
source_quote: The total cost of this projects is expected to reach AUD43 billion, with public investment expected to total AUD26 billion over eight years.

growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in renewable energy and clean technologies is expected to drive innovation and create jobs in the green sector.
status_evidence: The Clean Energy Legislative Package and the Clean Energy Futures Plan have been adopted and are in force.
importance_rationale: These reforms represent a significant commitment to green innovation and investment in the renewable energy sector.
description: The Clean Energy Legislative Package and the Clean Energy Futures Plan were adopted to promote innovation, commercialization, and investment in renewable energies and energy efficiency. The Clean Energy Finance Corporation is set to invest AUD 10 billion in renewable energy and clean technologies, supporting green innovation.
source_quote: The Clean Energy Futures Plan, aiming to boost green innovation, is another welcome initiative towards new commercialisation activity.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing the number of graduates is expected to enhance the skill level of the workforce, supporting economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform has been fully implemented, allowing for an uncapped number of Commonwealth-supported places.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the higher education funding model, which is crucial for addressing skill shortages.
description: The Australian government uncapped the number of Commonwealth-supported undergraduate places in 2012, transitioning from a capped allocation system to a demand-driven funding model. This reform aims to increase the number of young adults holding university degrees, thereby enhancing the higher education sector's responsiveness to economic needs.
source_quote: The government uncapped in 2012 the number of Commonwealth-supported undergraduate places.

growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms are designed to enhance the financial viability of SMEs and startups, promoting innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: Both reforms have been implemented, providing immediate financial relief and support for innovation.
importance_rationale: The transition to a tax credit system for R&D represents a major reform in how innovation is supported financially.
description: The Australian Government introduced a corporate loss carry-back reform, allowing firms to offset current-year losses against previously taxed profits, which is particularly beneficial for SMEs and innovative startups facing new challenges. Additionally, the R&D tax regime was transitioned from tax deductions to a tax credit, significantly increasing assistance rates for smaller firms and expanding access to foreign companies.
source_quote: The corporate loss carry-back reform will not only have beneficial effects on the level and quality of capital investment, but...may also bolster the automatic stabilisers in the economy.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between researchers and businesses is expected to lead to increased innovation and productivity.
status_evidence: The government is considering the provision of innovation vouchers and has set targets for collaboration, indicating these reforms are in the planning stage.
importance_rationale: The reform aims to significantly improve collaboration in the innovation system, which is a critical area for enhancing innovation performance.
description: The Australian government is considering the provision of well-designed innovation vouchers to facilitate effective connections between researchers and businesses, while also implementing programmes to support collaboration and networking between universities and businesses to enhance their impact on innovation. Additionally, the government has set a target to double the level of collaboration between researchers and businesses over the next decade, addressing the critical weakness of low collaboration in the innovation system.
source_quote: Consideration should be given, fiscal savings allowing, to further measures to increase collaboration between researchers and business, such as the provision of well-designed innovation vouchers.

growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms aim to improve education and training, thereby enhancing the skills of the workforce, which supports economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented as part of the government's education strategy.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant investments and structural changes in the education and training sectors.
description: The Australian government has initiated several reforms to enhance human capital, including universal access to early childhood education for all four-year-olds, a ten-year funding plan of AUD2.5 billion for trades training centres in secondary schools, and the introduction of a Productivity Places Programme to provide up to 630,000 training places over five years, focusing on high-level certificates and addressing skill shortages.
source_quote: More university places to train early childhood educators, along with the provision of financial incentives for graduates to work in areas of specific needs.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving coordination and strategic planning in science and innovation investments, ISA is expected to enhance the overall effectiveness of Australia's innovation system.
status_evidence: The relevant legislation for its establishment became effective in July 2016.
importance_rationale: The establishment of ISA represents a significant structural change aimed at improving the coherence and effectiveness of Australia's innovation system.
description: The establishment of Innovation and Science Australia (ISA) aims to provide strategic whole-of-government advice on science, research, and innovation, enhancing coordination across various government departments.
source_quote: A new statutory independent board, Innovation and Science Australia (ISA), was announced in December 2015, as part of the NISA, with responsibility for 'strategic whole-of-government advice on all science, research and innovation matters'.

growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to enhance the commercialization of research, fostering innovation and productivity in the startup sector.
status_evidence: The fund was launched at the end of 2016 to support early-stage commercialization.
importance_rationale: The establishment of the CSIRO Innovation Fund represents a significant investment in the commercialization of research, which is crucial for enhancing Australia's innovation ecosystem.
description: The CSIRO Innovation Fund was launched to support early-stage commercialization of research from CSIRO and other institutions, with a focus on co-investment in new spin-off and start-up companies.
source_quote: A new fund (the CSIRO Innovation Fund), announced as part of NISA, was launched at end-2016 by the government to support early stage commercialisation of research from CSIRO, other research institutions and universities.

growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted funding to bridge the gap between early and late-stage research, the fund enhances the commercialization potential of biomedical innovations, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: The fund is being financed by redirecting initial capital contributions from the Medical Research Future Fund.
importance_rationale: This fund represents a significant but targeted intervention to support the biomedical sector, which is crucial for innovation in health and medical research.
description: Australia has introduced a Biomedical Translation Fund to facilitate the transition of firms from early to late-stage biomedical research, addressing the so-called 'valley of death' in funding.
source_quote: This government-financed fund facilitates firms transition between early and late-stage biomedical research.

growth_orientation_rationale: By aligning funding with collaboration incentives, the reform is expected to enhance research quality and relevance, ultimately benefiting economic productivity.
status_evidence: The new funding system has been operational since early 2017.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how university research is funded, promoting collaboration and responsiveness, which are critical for enhancing innovation outcomes.
description: A new simplified funding system for university research began operating in early 2017, where federal block grants are determined solely by income from competitive grants and other sources, now given equal weight. This reform aims to increase universities' incentives to collaborate and make government competitive grants more responsive to applicants' needs.
source_quote: A new simplified funding system for university research began operating in early 2017, in which federal block grants for research are determined solely by income from two categories, competitive grants and other sources.

growth_orientation_rationale: The substantial investment is expected to boost R&D activities, leading to innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The funding has been allocated and is being utilized for various R&D initiatives.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a major structural change in public funding for R&D in Australia.
description: The Australian government announced a significant AUD 2.4 billion investment in research, science, and technology capabilities as part of the 2018-19 Budget, aligning with the recommendations from the report 'Australia 2030 Prosperity through Innovation'. This funding reform aims to enhance the overall research and development landscape in Australia.
source_quote: The government - under the Quality Schools package - will be gradually increasing the total funding and altering allocation formulae for federal-government grants to schools.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the R&D Tax Incentive, the government aims to stimulate private sector investment in R&D, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect, with adjustments made to the R&D Tax Incentive.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in how R&D tax incentives are structured and administered.
description: Reforms to the R&D Tax Incentive aim to enhance its effectiveness in promoting new R&D activities while improving program integrity and administration. The government has announced a refocus of the incentive towards supporting high-intensity R&D to better align with public funding objectives.
source_quote: Recent policy moves have included announced reforms to refocus the R&D Tax Incentive towards supporting high-intensity R&D.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating trials for autonomous vehicles can lead to advancements in transportation technology and urban mobility solutions.
status_evidence: The initiative has been proposed and is in the process of being developed.
importance_rationale: While significant, the initiative does not represent a major overhaul of transportation policy.
description: Australia is encouraging autonomous vehicle trials through legislative changes and partnerships, aiming to advance readiness for self-driving technology in urban environments.
source_quote: Legislation allowing automated vehicles trials passed 2018.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhances agricultural resilience to drought, thereby supporting productivity and sustainability in the sector.
component_name: On-farm Emergency Water Infrastructure Rebate Scheme
status_evidence: The scheme was launched with allocated funding for drought preparedness.
importance_rationale: The scheme represents a significant investment in agricultural infrastructure, aimed at enhancing resilience to climate change impacts.
description: The On-farm Emergency Water Infrastructure Rebate Scheme was launched to provide AUD 100 million in funding for farmers to improve drought preparedness through the installation of on-farm water infrastructure.
source_quote: The On-farm Emergency Water Infrastructure Rebate Scheme was launched in December 2018 and includes AUD 100 million in funding for farmers to improve drought preparedness.

growth_orientation_rationale: Expanding the innovation voucher programme is expected to foster collaboration between industry and researchers, leading to increased innovation and economic resilience.
status_evidence: The programme has been expanded and is currently operational as of 2019.
importance_rationale: The expansion of the innovation voucher programme represents a significant but narrower change aimed at enhancing innovation support.
description: The South Australian government intends to expand its innovation voucher programme as part of a broader plan to help the economy following the announced closure of car assembly plants, aiming to encourage collaboration between industry and researchers.
source_quote: The South Australian government intends to expand its voucher programme as part of a broader plan to help the economy following the announced closure of car assembly plants.

growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance urban infrastructure and technology, which can lead to increased productivity and improved living conditions.
status_evidence: The program has been actively providing funding for projects aimed at improving urban environments.
importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant investment in urban technology but does not constitute a major overhaul of existing systems.
description: The Australian government's 'Smart Cities Plan' aims to provide greater federal support and guidance for developing and implementing technological solutions to urban issues, including co-financing for technology-based projects through the 'Smart Cities and Suburbs Programme' with a commitment of AUD 28.5 million. This initiative focuses on smart investment, policy, and technology to improve urban environments.
source_quote: The federal government is implementing the 'Smart Cities Plan', which aims to provide greater federal-government support and steerage on developing and implementing technological solutions to infrastructure and other issues in urban areas.

growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to finance for smaller banks and non-bank lenders, the fund aims to lower funding costs and improve lending conditions for SMEs, thereby supporting innovation and growth.
status_evidence: The first securitisation involving the Fund was launched in early 2021.
importance_rationale: The establishment of the fund represents a significant step towards improving the financing landscape for SMEs, which is crucial for fostering innovation and entrepreneurship.
description: The Australian government established the Australian Business Securitisation Fund in 2019 to develop the market for securitised SME loans by investing in securitised assets and warehouses backed by loans issued by non-bank lenders and smaller banks.
source_quote: The government established the Australian Business Securitisation Fund in 2019 to develop the market by investing in securitised assets and in warehouses backed by loans issued by non-bank lenders and smaller banks.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving digital infrastructure and services, the reform aims to enhance efficiency in public service delivery and support innovation in the private sector.
status_evidence: Key initiatives of the Digital Business Plan were announced and funding allocated in September 2020.
importance_rationale: The Digital Business Plan represents a significant investment in digital infrastructure, which is essential for modernizing public services and fostering innovation.
description: The Government announced the AUD800 million Digital Business Plan to enhance digital government services, including the development of a Digital Identity system and the Modernising Business Registers program.
source_quote: In September 2020, the Government announced the AUD800m Digital Business Plan.

growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in clean technologies is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in carbon-intensive sectors, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: The roadmap has been released and outlines government investment plans.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in government policy towards targeted investment in clean technologies, which is crucial for meeting climate goals and fostering innovation.
description: The Australian government has released the Technology Investment Roadmap, which identifies key areas for technological development to reduce emissions, guiding AUD20 billion of government investment over the next decade.
source_quote: In 2020, the government released the Technology Investment Roadmap that identified five key areas where technological developments could reduce emissions in Australia’s most carbon-intensive sectors.

growth_orientation_rationale: This investment is expected to improve disaster management capabilities, thereby enhancing resilience and reducing economic losses from climate-related events.
status_evidence: The investment was included in the 2021-22 Budget.
importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant investment in infrastructure aimed at improving climate resilience, which is crucial for mitigating the economic impacts of natural disasters.
description: The Australian government announced an investment of AUD209.7 million to establish the Australian Climate Service, aimed at enhancing the country's capacity to manage and adapt to natural disasters.
source_quote: The government announced in the 2021-22 Budget investment of AUD209.7 million to establish the Australian Climate Service.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing skills and training for the unemployed can improve employability and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The government announced new training supports as part of the budget.
importance_rationale: The introduction of new training supports represents a significant step towards improving workforce skills, which is crucial for addressing unemployment and enhancing economic productivity.
description: The Australian government announced new training supports as part of the 2021-22 Federal Budget, including an extension of an apprentice wage subsidy and additional affordable training courses for young people.
source_quote: As part of the 2021-22 Federal Budget, the government announced some new training supports, including an extension of an apprentice wage subsidy.

growth_orientation_rationale: The patent box aims to attract and retain innovative firms in the medical and biotechnology sectors, promoting investment and job creation in high-value industries.
status_evidence: In the 2021-22 Budget the Government announced a patent box to encourage companies to develop and apply their medical and biotechnology innovations in Australia.
importance_rationale: While the patent box is a significant incentive for specific sectors, it is a targeted reform rather than a comprehensive overhaul of the innovation system.
description: The Government introduced a patent box to incentivize the development and application of medical and biotechnology innovations in Australia, taxing profits from these innovations at a concessional rate.
source_quote: In the 2021-22 Budget the Government announced a patent box to encourage companies to develop and apply their medical and biotechnology innovations in Australia.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving charging infrastructure is essential for increasing electric vehicle adoption, which can lead to reduced emissions and foster innovation in the automotive sector.
status_evidence: The federal government recently announced co-investment with the private sector.
importance_rationale: This investment is significant as it targets a critical barrier to electric vehicle adoption, which is essential for achieving broader environmental goals.
description: The federal government announced co-investment with the private sector from the AUD71.9 million Future Fuels Fund to address charging blackspots and demonstrate hydrogen refuelling infrastructure, aiming to enhance electric vehicle adoption.
source_quote: The federal government recently announced co-investment with the private sector from the AUD71.9 million Future Fuels Fund to address 'charging blackspots' and to demonstrate hydrogen refuelling infrastructure.

growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting high-skilled workers enhances the talent pool, which is crucial for driving innovation and productivity in the economy.
status_evidence: The government announced two new visas for high-skilled workers.
importance_rationale: This initiative is significant as it aims to enhance the skilled workforce, which is essential for fostering innovation and economic growth.
description: The Australian government announced two new visas for high-skilled workers to attract talent in innovation sectors, alongside plans to modernise the tax treatment of employee share schemes.
source_quote: In May 2021 the government announced two new visas for high-skilled workers and plans to modernise the tax treatment of employee share schemes.

growth_orientation_rationale: Easing regulations can improve access to capital for startups, fostering innovation and growth in the entrepreneurial sector.
status_evidence: The Committee has recommended adjusting public equity regulations to make it easier for company founders to scale back their investment.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses barriers to capital access for startups, which is crucial for their growth and innovation potential.
description: The Australian government is considering adjustments to public equity regulations to facilitate easier scaling back of investments by company founders, which could enhance access to capital for innovative startups.

source_quote: The government adopted the Working Agreement on Research and Technology Policy (1994-98) which incorporates many recommendations of the OECD Technology/Productivity Job Creation Study.

A technology package is being implemented to raise the level of R&D in Austria, promoting clusters in basic research and subsidizing the employment of scientists in industry.
source_quote: Major elements of the National Action Plan are: ... fostering research and innovation for the purpose of increasing the level of employment.

source_quote: Tax concessions for research and development aim at fostering the attractiveness of Austria as a business location and at promoting employment.

Additionally, the Industrial Research Promotion Fund has been established to facilitate joint programmes between polytechnics and enterprises, and competence centres are being created to integrate university and industry collaboration.
source_quote: The government has recently extended coverage so that individuals with a right to unemployment benefits will preserve their entitlement during a period of self-employment in case the enterprise fails.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the effectiveness of R&D funding is expected to lead to better outcomes in innovation and productivity, thereby supporting economic growth.

growth_orientation_rationale: These changes are expected to improve the quality of education and reduce dropout rates, leading to a more skilled workforce.

This involves ambitious initiatives to enhance the science infrastructure, technological capabilities of enterprises, and the human capital of researchers.
source_quote: A 'paradigm shift' about the contribution of innovation and innovation policies to growth is highly topical in Austria.

source_quote: The downsides of the early streaming of students into 'academic' and 'general' routes at the excessively young age of 10 are now recognised in Austria.

source_quote: The government also intends to strengthen the Polytechnische Schulen.

source_quote: An integrated policy framework to make Austria more gender-equal should build on 3 pillars: i) make the tax and benefit system more employment friendly; ii) extend the care service infrastructure; and iii) encourage more flexible workplace practices.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to stimulate innovation and investment, thereby enhancing economic growth.

importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant overhaul of investment incentives, aimed at fostering innovation and attracting foreign investment.
description: In 1998, the Czech government introduced a comprehensive set of investment incentives aimed at attracting foreign direct investment and enhancing innovation. This included the abolition of tax holidays in favor of activity-specific allowances and credits for expenditure on new plant and machinery, as well as relief from customs duties and subsidies for employee education. The programme was further enhanced in 1999 to lower the threshold for support and increase job-creation grants.
source_quote: In 1998, the authorities introduced a range of incentives including relief from customs duties for certain kinds of investment goods.

source_quote: The programme, which has yet to be approved, seeks to significantly improve economic conditions in 13 high-unemployment regions; to support small and medium enterprises; to enhance the domestic manufacturing sector’s performance.

growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in R&D is expected to enhance innovation capacity and competitiveness in key sectors, thereby supporting economic growth.

growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating the employment of older workers, the initiative enhances labor market participation and utilizes the skills of an experienced workforce, contributing positively to economic growth.

source_quote: The government has launched a series of measures called the 'Initiative 50plus'... including wage subsidies amounting to between 30% and 50% of the wages paid for up to 36 months.

source_quote: The recent amendment Act increases the amount of individual support significantly (10% or more) from autumn 2008 and will also increase the number of students supported.

growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in energy research and infrastructure is expected to enhance innovation in the energy sector and support the transition to renewable energy.

status_evidence: The investment and funding have been executed.

This investment is part of the 6th Energy Research Programme, which allocates EUR 3.5 billion for energy research from 2011 to 2014, marking a 75% increase compared to previous funding periods.

source_quote: Reform of bankruptcy legislation, most recently in 2012, reduced the stay period after which bankrupt entrepreneurs are cleared from remaining debt.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving procurement efficiency can lead to better allocation of resources and stimulate innovation in the public sector.

status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in the legal framework supporting entrepreneurship.
description: The German government has implemented reforms to encourage entrepreneurship by reducing the discharge period for bankrupt entrepreneurs from six years to three years, provided they repay a portion of their debt. Additionally, to facilitate start-ups, the government plans to introduce a 'One-Stop-Shop' and a VAT exemption for the first years after starting a business. A legislation expanding the options for carrying forward tax losses was also adopted in 2016, aimed at facilitating the financial management of start-ups.
source_quote: To facilitate start-ups, the government plans to introduce a 'One-Stop-Shop' and a VAT exemption for the first years after starting a business.

source_quote: A new market segment in Deutsche Börse for young high growth firms ('Scale') was launched in 2017.

source_quote: The Act on Opportunities to Gain Qualifications, in effect since 2019, has expanded training opportunities for individuals independent of age, qualification and firm size.

These measures aim to enhance research partnerships and stimulate private sector investment in innovation.
source_quote: A tax credit scheme was tried out in 2002 whereby a firm received a 150% tax credit for payments made to a public institution as part of a collaborative research project.

This reform aims to improve technology transfer to enhance the commercialization of research, addressing previous bureaucratic obstacles.
source_quote: A reform in 2000 improved technology transfer, but research funding is still largely institution-based with little explicit link to quality.

The government plans to shift towards a contestable performance-based funding mechanism for research, allowing private firms to compete for public research funds on equal terms, which is expected to improve the effectiveness of the research budget by tying funding to quality outcomes.
source_quote: Getting better results from the research budget would also be useful. One way is to shift further towards a contestable performance-based funding mechanism and to tie funding to quality.

This includes attracting private investment, enhancing public-private cooperation, and focusing on missions such as carbon capture, power-to-X, and eco-friendly agriculture. Major programmes like the Energy Technology Development and Demonstration Programme (EUDP) are central to this effort.
source_quote: The green research strategy is focused on four missions: 1) carbon capture, utilisation and storage, 2) power-to-X, 3) climate and eco-friendly agriculture and food production, 4) recycling and reduction of plastic waste.

source_quote: The research and development tax subsidy remains relatively low by international standards. Further increase public support to research and development by raising the R&D tax subsidy and making it incremental.

Additionally, the new National Plan for Science, Technology and Innovation 2017-20 aims to consolidate information on all support schemes from different institutions, enhancing transparency and accessibility for innovative firms.

Additionally, training vouchers for the unemployed were approved to fund training programs of their choice, aiming to improve the matching between participants and training centres. The government is also developing a new Digital Strategy for Smart Spain to ensure that the availability of digital skills complements the needs of firms, especially at the regional level.
