growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between public research and industry is expected to drive innovation and improve productivity in key sectors.
package_name: National Science and Technology Development Initiative
status_evidence: The government should focus on promoting mechanisms for public/private research co-operation.
importance_rationale: This initiative aims to bridge the gap between research institutions and industry, which is crucial for enhancing innovation capacity in Mexico.
description: The Mexican government is promoting mechanisms for public/private research cooperation to stimulate the development and application of technology across various sectors, including agriculture and resource-related industries.
source_quote: The Mexican government should focus on promoting mechanisms for public/private research co-operation (including decentralisation of R&D institutions), with domestic as well as foreign firms.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the skills of workers in micro and small enterprises, the initiative is expected to enhance productivity and innovation in these sectors.
status_evidence: The Ministry of Labour has expanded the scale of training programmes focused on micro and small enterprises.
importance_rationale: This expansion of training programmes is significant for enhancing the skills of a large segment of the workforce, which is vital for economic growth.
description: The Ministry of Labour has expanded the scale of training programmes focused on micro and small enterprises, aiming to enhance skills and competencies in the workforce.
source_quote: The Ministry of Labour has expanded the scale of training programmes focused on micro and small enterprises (CIMO), which is appropriate.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public funding for R&D and infrastructure enhances the capacity for innovation and industrial development, leading to higher productivity.
status_evidence: The government actively funds R&D and supports technology infrastructure.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant commitment to enhancing R&D funding and infrastructure, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic growth.
description: The government funds more than 71% of total R&D spending and supports industrial technology by providing science and technology infrastructure, assisting industrial development in institutes and universities, and supporting the diffusion process of innovations.
source_quote: The government funds more than 71% of total R&D spending and industry 17%.

growth_orientation_rationale: R&D tax credits incentivize private sector investment in research and development, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: Tax credits for R&D expenditures have been on the rise during the 1990s.
importance_rationale: The introduction of R&D tax credits represents a significant policy shift aimed at stimulating private sector R&D investment.
description: Tax credits for R&D expenditures have been on the rise during the 1990s in Mexico, aligning with trends observed in other OECD countries.
source_quote: All OECD countries (except New Zealand) have special tax schemes for R&D expenditure, and most of them offer various types of R&D tax credits.

growth_orientation_rationale: By restructuring CFE into smaller autonomous units, the initiative aims to foster competition and improve efficiency in the electricity sector.
status_evidence: The collective experience of other OECD countries shows that best practice reform of state-owned electricity sectors creates multiple generators privatised to separate owners.
importance_rationale: The reorganisation of CFE is a significant step towards improving the efficiency of the electricity sector, although it may not be sufficient to instigate real competitive behaviour.
description: The ongoing reorganisation of the Comisión Federal de Electricidad (CFE) aims to create a 'shadow' energy market where all participants function under the same commercial principles, facilitating competition.
source_quote: The ongoing reorganisation has started at CFE (approved by the CFE board in October 1999).

growth_orientation_rationale: The proposed reforms aim to enhance competition and efficiency in the electricity market, which could lead to lower prices and improved service delivery.
status_evidence: The government proposed a series of reforms in February 1999, but these have not been approved by Congress.
importance_rationale: The proposed restructuring of the electricity sector represents a significant shift towards liberalisation and private sector involvement, which is crucial for enhancing efficiency and competition in a key economic sector.
description: The government proposed a series of reforms in February 1999 aimed at restructuring the electricity sector, including the establishment of a new legal and regulatory framework to facilitate private initiative in power generation and sales.
source_quote: The government proposed a series of reforms in February 1999, but these have not been approved by Congress.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving access to knowledge and education is expected to enhance human capital and productivity.
status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: While significant, the reform is not a structural overhaul but rather an enhancement of existing educational opportunities.
description: The Mexican government aims to give citizens opportunities to access knowledge and education through the intelligent use of technologies, including reforms to support the use of electronic and IT media.
source_quote: Give Mexicans opportunities to access knowledge, learning and education by means of an intelligent use of technologies.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating private sector entry into electricity generation is expected to enhance efficiency and innovation in the sector.
status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the electricity sector.
description: The Mexican government proposed a new regulation for the electricity sector to create market conditions, allowing private producers to enter the generation market while maintaining state control over transmission and distribution.
source_quote: The reform proposed by the previous administration in 1999... was not approved by Congress. In 2002, the current administration submitted a new proposal to Congress, which is less ambitious than the previous one.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reform aims to enhance productivity and innovation within firms, which is expected to lead to improved economic efficiency and growth.
status_evidence: The reform was submitted to Congress but has not yet been implemented.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of public finance and innovation policy, indicating a major shift in approach.
description: A comprehensive public finance reform was submitted to Congress in June 2007, aiming to strengthen tax administration, improve spending efficiency through systematic impact evaluation, and reduce dependence on oil revenue, while also emphasizing the need for reforms to promote within-firm productivity growth and innovation.
source_quote: The government submitted to Congress in June 2007 a comprehensive public finance reform, which includes four main pillars.

growth_orientation_rationale: Providing resources and support to micro and small enterprises can enhance their productivity and contribute to economic growth.
package_name: Support for Micro and Small Enterprises
status_evidence: The program has been developed and is operational.
importance_rationale: The Mexico Emprende program represents a significant initiative to support small businesses, though it is not as comprehensive as major reforms.
description: Mexico has developed the Mexico Emprende program, which provides web-based information and business centers to assist micro, small, and medium-sized enterprises in enhancing their productivity and formalizing their operations.
source_quote: Mexico has developed these kinds of services through its programme Mexico Emprende with web-based information and a number of business centres that attend micro as well as small and medium-sized enterprises throughout the country.

growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining business registration processes encourages entrepreneurship and formalization, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The government has announced plans for the integration of SARE with tuempresa.gob.mx.
importance_rationale: The integration of SARE with tuempresa.gob.mx represents a significant but narrower reform aimed at improving the business environment.
description: Mexico has made significant progress in easing procedures to start a business through the launch of the Sistema de Apertura Rápida de Empresas (SARE), which has streamlined registration processes for low-risk businesses. Additionally, the government plans to integrate SARE with tuempresa.gob.mx, an electronic one-stop shop to ease compliance with federal business start-up regulations, significantly reducing the time required for registration.
source_quote: Mexico has made significant progress in easing procedures to start a business, including through the launch in 2002 of the Sistema de Apertura Rápida de Empresas (SARE).

growth_orientation_rationale: Reducing hiring and firing costs can encourage formal employment, which supports economic growth.
status_evidence: The labor reform proposal has been announced but not yet legislated.
importance_rationale: The labor reform proposal represents a significant change aimed at improving labor market conditions.
description: A recent labor reform proposal in Mexico aims to limit the accumulation of due wages during labor trials, which could reduce the costs and uncertainties associated with hiring and firing, potentially encouraging formal employment.
source_quote: The reform is still under discussion in Congress, but if it were implemented along the lines of the original proposal, this would ease the burden of employment protection legislation quite substantially.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance funding for innovation and climate change, which are expected to raise productivity and support economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been officially announced but are not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: Both reforms represent significant structural changes in funding mechanisms for climate and R&D.
description: The Mexican government has announced the establishment of a Climate Fund under the General Law on Climate Change to collect and channel public and private resources for climate-change interventions, while also committing to raise R&D intensity to 1% of GDP to strengthen public funding for the business sector and enhance the innovation environment.
source_quote: The General Law on Climate Change establishes the creation of a Climate Fund for the collection and channelling of public and private resources for mitigation and adaptation actions.

growth_orientation_rationale: The establishment of the commission is expected to enhance policy coordination, which can lead to improved productivity and innovation.
status_evidence: The commission has been announced and is in the early stages of operation.
importance_rationale: The establishment of the commission represents a significant step towards better policy coordination but is not a major overhaul.
description: The new National Productivity Commission has been established to improve coordination and identify policy reforms, showing early signs of effective operation.
source_quote: As a central part of the Programme to Democratise Productivity, the new National Productivity Commission has been established to improve coordination and identify policy reforms.

growth_orientation_rationale: Supporting clean energy initiatives is expected to foster innovation in the energy sector and contribute to sustainable development.
package_name: Energy Reform for Clean Energy Deployment
component_name: Clean Energy Certificates and Auction Prices
status_evidence: The reform measures are currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant shift towards clean energy and regulatory changes in the energy sector.
description: Mexico's energy reform includes support for the deployment of clean energies through mechanisms that require electricity retailers to acquire clean energy certificates and allows suppliers to sell clean energy at fixed auction prices.
source_quote: Mexico's energy reform also provides support for the deployment of 'clean' energies, through two mechanisms.

growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining regulatory procedures is expected to facilitate entrepreneurship and innovation.
status_evidence: The reform has been officially announced but is not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: The implementation of a comprehensive regulatory framework represents a major reform in supporting entrepreneurship.
description: Mexico has taken important steps to reduce barriers to entrepreneurship and streamline regulatory procedures, including the implementation of the General Law of Regulatory Improvement, which aims to extend reforms to the subnational level and create a comprehensive database of business administrative procedures.
source_quote: Mexico has taken important steps to reduce barriers to entrepreneurship and streamline regulatory procedures, including by making more use of electronic platforms.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving funding mechanisms for sustainable transport, the reform is expected to enhance urban mobility and reduce environmental impacts, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: The program is currently under revision with expected changes.
importance_rationale: The revision of PROTRAM represents a significant adjustment to improve urban mobility funding, which is crucial for sustainable development in urban areas.
description: The governance and operational rules of the Federal Support Programme for Massive Transport (PROTRAM) are under revision to extend support to smaller municipalities and include transport electrification projects, enhancing the capacity for sustainable urban mobility.
source_quote: The governance and operational rules of the urban sustainable mobility program (PROTRAM) are currently under revision.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing innovation capacity is expected to improve productivity and create jobs, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: The Government's priority is the creation of regular jobs in the private sector, emphasizing innovation.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy focus towards innovation and competitiveness, aiming for substantial job creation and economic growth.
description: The new Government has emphasized the need to enhance innovation capacity to address challenges posed by technological change and increased trade competition, focusing on strengthening competitiveness and entrepreneurship.
source_quote: The new Government which took office in August 1994 has stressed the need to make the whole economy more flexible and dynamic in order to step up non-inflationary economic growth and create sufficient jobs.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing skills and qualifications is essential for boosting productivity and reducing mismatches in the labor market, which can lead to higher economic growth.
status_evidence: The measures to upgrade skills and competences have been actively pursued.
importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change aimed at improving the overall skill level of the workforce, which is crucial for long-term economic competitiveness.
description: The Netherlands has implemented measures to increase the knowledge base, efficiency, and innovation capacity of its workforce by upgrading skills and competences through education and training programs.
source_quote: Increasing the knowledge base, efficiency and innovation capacity by upgrading skills and competences is necessary to boost the non-inflationary rate of growth of the economy.

growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, thereby improving employability and productivity in the labor market.
status_evidence: The tax advantage for firms employing apprentices was introduced at the beginning of 1996.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly targets the development of human capital in the workforce, which is crucial for economic growth.
description: The Dutch government introduced a tax advantage for firms employing apprentices, aiming to increase the number of apprentices by 18,000 over three years.
source_quote: A tax advantage for firms employing apprentices was introduced at the beginning of 1996.

growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing private research spending, the measures are expected to enhance innovation capacity and contribute to economic growth.
status_evidence: The Government has taken a large number of initiatives directed to increasing private research spending.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in policy aimed at enhancing the innovation landscape in the Netherlands, with potential long-term impacts on economic performance.
description: The Government has initiated measures to increase private research spending through tax incentives and government-sponsored loans for high-risk development projects.
source_quote: The Government has taken a large number of initiatives directed to increasing private research spending, through tax incentives and government-sponsored loans for high-risk development projects.

growth_orientation_rationale: This tax incentive is designed to stimulate R&D investment among small businesses, which is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The WBSO has been successfully implemented and is actively promoting R&D investment.
importance_rationale: The WBSO represents a major reform in the funding landscape for R&D, significantly altering the incentives for small businesses.
description: The Dutch government initiated the WBSO (Act to promote R&D), which provides tax reductions on labor costs for R&D staff, aimed at increasing investment in R&D among small businesses.
source_quote: The WBSO (Act to promote R&D) grants a tax reduction on labour cost of R&D staff.

growth_orientation_rationale: By fostering technological cooperation and innovation clusters, this policy aims to enhance collaboration and drive economic growth.
status_evidence: The expansion of the cluster policy has been put into effect as part of the government's innovation strategy.
importance_rationale: The expansion of cluster policy represents a significant effort to enhance innovation ecosystems.
description: The Dutch government is expanding its cluster policy to promote technological cooperation and the emergence of innovative clusters and networks of businesses and research institutes.
source_quote: The emphasis in technology policy has shifted to promoting technological co-operation and the emergence of innovative clusters and networks.

growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to enhance R&D investment and facilitate the commercialization of innovations, which are expected to boost productivity and economic growth.
component_name: R&D Tax Incentives and Patent Streamlining
status_evidence: The measures have been put into effect as part of the government's ongoing efforts to enhance R&D and innovation.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in the fiscal and regulatory environment for R&D and innovation, but do not constitute a complete overhaul of the system.
description: The Dutch government has introduced fiscal measures to promote research and development, including tax reductions on labor costs for R&D staff and streamlined patenting processes to encourage firms to seek patent protection for their innovations. Additionally, institutional and tax reforms are proposed to encourage financial companies to provide risk capital and support the commercialization of private research.
source_quote: The government has proposed to introduce institutional and tax reforms in order to give financial companies both the scope for action and the incentive to play a more dynamic role in the Dutch economy – i.e. to provide risk capital and support the commercialisation of private research.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing collaboration between academia and industry, this initiative aims to drive innovation and economic growth through more relevant research outputs.
status_evidence: The efforts to improve linkages have been put into effect as part of the government's innovation strategy.
importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to strengthen the connection between research and market needs.
description: The Dutch government has initiated efforts to improve linkages between academic research and the business sector, including new incentive structures to ensure research is more demand-driven.
source_quote: Further efforts to find ways of commercialising the results of academic work seem warranted.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to advice and support for start-ups, this initiative is expected to foster entrepreneurship and innovation, contributing positively to economic growth.
status_evidence: The initiative has been officially announced and is set to be implemented nationally.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant improvement in the support structure for start-ups, although it is not a complete overhaul of the system.
description: The Dutch government has concentrated its agencies providing first-line advice to start-ups into 'Enterprise Houses', which will be implemented nationally to enhance service delivery at the local level.
source_quote: The concentration of government agencies providing first-line advice to start-ups into 'Enterprise Houses', to be applied nationally from the beginning of 1998, should also provide better delivery of these services at the local level.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance risk capital availability and reduce barriers to entrepreneurship, fostering innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice and are actively supporting innovation and entrepreneurship in the Netherlands.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant structural changes aimed at enhancing the innovation ecosystem and supporting entrepreneurship.
description: The Dutch authorities introduced institutional and tax reforms to enhance the supply of risk capital and support the commercialization of private research while implementing measures to reduce impediments to entrepreneurship, including phasing out the Establishment Law for certain self-employed sectors and streamlining tax requirements to enhance technological start-ups.
source_quote: The authorities are taking measures to reduce impediments to entrepreneurship, such as high administrative burdens, complicated subsidy schemes, and growth barriers for potential high-growth companies.

growth_orientation_rationale: Aligning research infrastructure with societal needs can enhance the effectiveness of R&D investments and drive innovation.
status_evidence: The government has already taken the initiative to improve the match between this infrastructure and society’s knowledge requirements.
importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it aims to enhance the alignment of research efforts with societal needs, which is crucial for effective innovation.
description: The government has taken steps to improve the match between public research infrastructure and society’s knowledge requirements through the introduction of target funding from the Netherlands Organisation for Applied Scientific Research (TNO) and the Large Technological Institutes (GTIs).
source_quote: The government has already taken the initiative to improve the match between this infrastructure and society’s knowledge requirements.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing innovation and productivity is expected to raise overall economic performance and competitiveness in the long run.
status_evidence: The government has identified key challenges and is actively prioritizing science and technology policy.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in focus towards enhancing innovation and productivity, which are critical for the Dutch economy's future growth.
description: The government is prioritizing science and technology policy to enhance innovation and productivity growth, addressing low business sector R&D spending and the need for better interaction between public and private sectors.
source_quote: The government is giving high priority to science and technology policy.

growth_orientation_rationale: The initiative aims to enhance the growth potential of the startup ecosystem and facilitate entrepreneurship by reducing barriers and risks associated with failure.
status_evidence: The TechnoPartner Action Programme has been established and bankruptcy law reforms are in effect.
importance_rationale: The establishment of the TechnoPartner Action Programme and significant bankruptcy law reforms represent a major overhaul of support for startups.
description: The Dutch government established the TechnoPartner Action Programme to streamline existing instruments supporting technology-based start-ups and plans to reform bankruptcy law to reduce personal costs for bankrupt entrepreneurs, facilitating a quicker restart for non-fraudulent bankrupts, while also enhancing public support for innovative firms.
source_quote: A new law on bankruptcy will be available at the end of 2005. As is the government’s intention, this law should reduce the personal costs of bankruptcy and the options for a swift re-start of non-fraudulent bankrupts.

growth_orientation_rationale: This scheme aims to enhance the supply of skilled labor in knowledge-intensive sectors, thereby fostering innovation and productivity growth.
status_evidence: The scheme has been increasingly used, with nearly 4000 entry visas granted to knowledge workers in 2006.
importance_rationale: The introduction of this scheme represents a significant policy change aimed at attracting skilled labor, which is crucial for enhancing the innovation capacity of the Dutch economy.
description: In 2004, the Netherlands introduced a scheme to attract highly-skilled workers, exempting those earning over €45,000 from the work permit requirement, aimed at stimulating innovation in the economy.
source_quote: In 2004, a scheme to attract highly-skilled workers was introduced following the advice of the Innovation Platform, established by the government to design policies aimed at stimulating innovation in the Dutch economy.

growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing access to affordable childcare, the reform encourages higher female labor force participation, which can enhance overall productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform was enacted to improve the flexibility and affordability of childcare services.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in childcare policy, aiming to enhance labor market participation and address barriers faced by working parents, particularly women.
description: The Dutch government enacted a reform of the childcare subsidy system in 2005, allowing for a more flexible supply of childcare services by providing income-dependent benefits to households purchasing these services, rather than subsidizing providers directly.
source_quote: A reform of the childcare subsidy system was enacted in 2005 by the central government to allow a more flexible supply of childcare services.

growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory barriers, the reform is expected to enhance entrepreneurship and stimulate the creation of new businesses, contributing to economic growth.
status_evidence: The law will be entirely abolished by January 2006, at the latest.
importance_rationale: The complete abolition of the Establishment Law represents a significant structural change aimed at fostering a more dynamic entrepreneurial environment, which is crucial for economic growth.
description: The Dutch government is phasing out the Establishment Law to reduce barriers to entrepreneurship, aiming for complete abolition by January 2006.
source_quote: The scope of the Establishment Law was substantially reduced at the beginning of this year, and the Law will be entirely abolished by January 2006, at the latest.

growth_orientation_rationale: The tax facility is designed to encourage collaboration between startups and universities, fostering innovation.
status_evidence: The TechnoPartner tax facility has been introduced and is currently operational.
importance_rationale: The introduction of the TechnoPartner tax facility represents a significant but narrower reform aimed at supporting startups.
description: The introduction of TechnoPartner, a generic tax facility for innovative startups collaborating with universities, aims to enhance cooperation and support for innovation in the startup ecosystem.
source_quote: Introduce TechnoPartner, the generic tax facility for innovative start-ups co-operating with universities.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improved coordination and targeted funding are expected to enhance the effectiveness of public funding for innovation and foster collaboration between universities and firms.
status_evidence: The rationalisation and reallocation of funds have been executed and are currently in effect.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment in funding strategy aimed at enhancing innovation collaboration.
description: The government has rationalised its financial support for R&D activity to improve coordination among various agencies and is reallocating part of the university research budget to incentivize universities to cooperate more with firms in innovation activities.
source_quote: A small step towards making university budgets more dependent on co-operation with firms in innovation activities is being taken by reallocating part of the university research budget (€100 million out of a total budget of €2 billion).

growth_orientation_rationale: Simplifying administrative procedures is expected to lower barriers for startups, thereby enhancing entrepreneurship and innovation.
status_evidence: The programme and Taskforce have been established and are actively working to simplify procedures.
importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant reform aimed at improving the business environment for startups.
description: The Dutch government initiated the 'Andere Overheid' programme aimed at simplifying administrative procedures and established a Taskforce to simplify licence and permit procedures, particularly focusing on reducing the complexity and costs associated with starting a business.
source_quote: The Andere Overheid programme focuses on less regulation, better public services, greater efficiency and better cooperation with private organisations.

growth_orientation_rationale: Focusing on key fields is expected to maximize the impact of R&D investments and drive innovation in critical sectors.
status_evidence: The shift in support instruments has been enacted and is currently operational.
importance_rationale: The shift in innovation support represents a major reform in how R&D budgets are allocated and utilized.
description: The Dutch government is shifting its innovation support towards specific instruments to enhance the external benefits of its R&D budget, focusing on key fields such as genomics, ICT, and nanotechnology.
source_quote: The Dutch government is moving more of its support for innovation towards specific instruments.

growth_orientation_rationale: The focus on excellent basic research and its valorisation is expected to contribute to long-term innovation and economic growth.
package_name: Smart Mix Policy for Basic Research
status_evidence: The policy instrument has been introduced but not yet fully implemented.
importance_rationale: The introduction of the smart mix policy represents a significant adjustment in funding strategy for basic research.
description: The Dutch government introduced the smart mix policy instrument to enhance focus on excellent basic research and its social and economic valorisation through collaborative programmes.
source_quote: A new policy instrument (the smart mix) will be launched in 2006 that provides for programmes to enhance focus and mass in excellent basic research and social and economic valorisation of this research.

growth_orientation_rationale: Linking funding to knowledge diffusion encourages universities to enhance their collaboration with industry, thereby promoting innovation.
status_evidence: Funding has been redistributed based on performance metrics.
importance_rationale: This reform fundamentally changes the funding model for universities, incentivizing them to engage more with industry and enhance their role in innovation.
description: The government has made university funding partly dependent on performance in the diffusion of knowledge to firms, redistributing €50 million of basic funding based on scientific performance and valorisation of knowledge gains.
source_quote: In 2006 €50 million of the basic funding of universities was redistributed dependent on scientific performance and valorisation of knowledge gains.

growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in tax allowance is expected to incentivize additional R&D spending by SMEs, thereby enhancing innovation and productivity.
status_evidence: The government has decided to step up the tax allowance by €100 million as of 2007.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant increase in financial support for R&D, particularly benefiting SMEs, which are crucial for innovation.
description: The government has decided to step up the tax allowance for R&D by €100 million as of 2007 to further increase private R&D, particularly among SMEs, which have shown a positive response to the existing WBSO tax scheme.
source_quote: To increase private R&D further, the government has decided to step up the tax allowance by €100 million as of 2007.

growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the rationalisation is not yet clear as it is still in the planning phase.
status_evidence: The rationalisation has been announced but not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: The planned rationalisation represents a significant overhaul of the support system for entrepreneurship.
description: The Ministry of Economic Affairs plans to rationalise the list of support instruments for entrepreneurship, which includes various innovation instruments such as Knowledge Transfer Subsidy Schemes and Innovation Vouchers.
source_quote: The MEA plans to rationalise the list of support instruments.

growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating access to finance, the scheme supports the growth and sustainability of SMEs, which are crucial for economic recovery and innovation.
status_evidence: The maximum loan amount and guarantee percentage were increased to support SMEs.
importance_rationale: The expansion of the BMKB scheme represents a significant adjustment to existing financial support mechanisms for SMEs, enhancing their ability to secure necessary funding.
description: The Dutch government expanded the SME loan guarantee scheme (BMKB) to increase the maximum loan amount and guarantee coverage, aimed at improving access to bank lending for small and medium-sized enterprises with limited collateral.
source_quote: The maximum loan amount of the SME loan guarantee scheme was increased from EUR 1 million to EUR 1.5 million per enterprise.

growth_orientation_rationale: By maintaining employment during downturns, the programme helps preserve human capital and supports firms' recovery, contributing to overall economic stability.
status_evidence: The reduced working hour programme has been successfully implemented to support firms.
importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it addresses employment challenges during economic downturns but does not represent a structural change.
description: The government introduced a reduced working hour programme to support firms facing temporary declines in activity, allowing them to maintain employment while reducing labor costs.
source_quote: The government-sponsored reduced working hour programme has lowered the cost of labour hoarding by allowing companies with significant and temporary declines in activity to put partly redundant workers on unemployment benefits for the hours they do not work.

growth_orientation_rationale: The R&D tax rebate is designed to stimulate innovation and research activities, which can enhance productivity and drive economic growth.
status_evidence: The broader R&D tax rebate has been successfully implemented to support companies during the economic crisis.
importance_rationale: The reform is significant as it expands existing tax incentives to support R&D during a crisis, but it does not represent a major overhaul.
description: The government introduced a broader R&D tax rebate to provide liquidity to companies during the economic crisis, aimed at stimulating innovation and research activities.
source_quote: The measures... included a broader R&D tax rebate, abolishing the air flight tax, energy investment tax credit, various tax cuts for SMEs.

growth_orientation_rationale: These measures aim to reduce credit constraints for innovative firms, thereby enhancing their ability to invest and grow, which supports overall economic growth.
status_evidence: The government has actively launched and supported these programmes since 2009.
importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant structural change in the financing landscape for SMEs, addressing critical market failures and enhancing access to capital for innovation.
description: The Dutch government launched several programmes to ease access to finance for SMEs and start-ups, including higher guarantees for bank lending and the establishment of a microcredit institution, Qredits.
source_quote: The government launched a number of programmes to ease access to finance during the crisis.

growth_orientation_rationale: While aimed at improving efficiency, the impact on growth is uncertain as it may lead to cuts in innovation funding.
status_evidence: The groups presented their results on April 1st, 2010.
importance_rationale: The establishment of study groups is a procedural reform aimed at identifying savings rather than a direct innovation policy change.
description: The government established 20 study groups to review spending and fiscal expenditures, focusing on areas including innovation and applied research to identify potential structural savings.
source_quote: The government established 20 study groups in key policy areas to review spending and fiscal expenditures in its each of the areas.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are designed to stimulate business investment and enhance the overall business environment, which is expected to raise productivity and innovation.
status_evidence: The policy was introduced and is currently in force.
importance_rationale: The reforms represent a structural change in the innovation policy framework, significantly impacting R&D incentives and business competitiveness.
description: The Dutch government is implementing a comprehensive innovation framework aimed at enhancing business sector competitiveness and innovation. This includes replacing direct subsidies with broader tax incentives, establishing 'innovation contracts' for nine key sectors, and reducing regulatory burdens to stimulate private R&D spending and improve access to risk capital.
source_quote: In February 2011, the government presented a new policy to foster business sector competitiveness and prepare the economy to face challenges and opportunities from globalisation.

growth_orientation_rationale: Focusing R&D efforts on key sectors is expected to enhance innovation and economic performance in those areas.
status_evidence: The approach has been put into practice and is currently operational.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant strategic shift in prioritizing R&D in specific sectors.
description: The Dutch government is reforming its policies for the business sector by implementing a 'top sector approach' aimed at improving framework conditions and prioritizing research and development activities in nine areas of excellence.
source_quote: The government is reforming the policies for the business sector... around two pillars... to focus on nine areas of excellence in the form of a 'top sector approach'.

growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to enhance R&D investment in SMEs, thereby fostering innovation and economic growth.
status_evidence: The fund has been operational since its launch in January 2012.
importance_rationale: The SME+ Innovation Fund represents a significant structural change in how the government supports innovation financing for SMEs, addressing a critical equity gap.
description: The Dutch government launched the 'SME+ Innovation Fund' to support innovative and fast-growing SMEs by mobilizing private capital and providing direct 'Innovation Credits' for R&D projects.
source_quote: In January 2012, the government launched the 'SME+ Innovation Fund'. The Fund is an umbrella for different (partly existing) innovation finance schemes.

growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing R&D through tax credits, the reform aims to boost private sector innovation and investment.
status_evidence: The plan has been publicly stated but not yet implemented.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how innovation is funded in the private sector.
description: The government plans to replace various direct innovation subsidies with R&D tax incentives, projected to more than double in fiscal costs, to stimulate innovative activities across the private sector.
source_quote: To increase innovative activities, various direct innovation subsidies will be replaced by R&D tax incentives.

growth_orientation_rationale: By promoting renewable energy, the agreement is expected to drive innovation in clean technologies and contribute to sustainable economic growth.
status_evidence: The National Energy Agreement includes measures that have been adopted to step up renewable energy production.
importance_rationale: The agreement represents a comprehensive approach to energy policy, aiming for significant advancements in renewable energy and sustainability, which are critical for long-term economic resilience.
description: The National Energy Agreement aims to increase renewable energy production in the Netherlands, raising the target for renewable energy to 16% by 2023 and emphasizing the development of wind energy.
source_quote: The National Energy Agreement of 2013 includes several measures to step up renewable energy production, while raising the target to 16% by 2023.

growth_orientation_rationale: This approach aims to enhance R&D and innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The two-pillar approach has been launched to promote innovation.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the innovation policy framework, aiming to significantly enhance R&D and innovation across key sectors.
description: The Netherlands has launched a two-pillar approach to promote a healthy entrepreneurial system with innovation at its core, focusing on enhancing framework conditions for the business sector and developing sector-specific policies to boost R&D in nine 'top sectors'.
source_quote: The Netherlands has launched an approach based on two complementary pillars to promote a healthy entrepreneurial system with innovation at its core.

growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining support services is expected to enhance accessibility and efficiency for SMEs, fostering innovation and growth.
status_evidence: The local and national Chambers of Commerce were merged into a single organisation.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the support system for SMEs, which is crucial for enhancing their growth and innovation capacity.
description: The government has launched a rationalisation of its support network for businesses by merging local and national Chambers of Commerce with the Syntens Innovation Centre into a single organisation, aimed at promoting the development of SMEs.
source_quote: The government has also launched a welcome rationalisation of its support network for businesses, which should further promote the development of SMEs.

growth_orientation_rationale: This initiative aims to leverage private investment in innovative start-ups, enhancing their growth potential and contribution to the economy.
component_name: Early Stage Instrument and Co-Investment Facility
status_evidence: The government has announced the addition of an early stage instrument and a co-investment facility for business angels.
importance_rationale: This addition enhances the existing SME+ Innovation Fund, providing targeted support for early-stage innovative firms.
description: The government announced an addition to the SME+ Innovation Fund, introducing an early stage instrument and a co-investment facility for business angels to support innovative start-ups.
source_quote: Recently, the government has announced an addition to the SME+ Innovation Fund of an early stage instrument and a co-investment facility for business angels.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increased funding for innovation in SMEs is expected to enhance productivity and competitiveness, contributing positively to economic growth.
package_name: SME Innovation Promotion Regions and Top Sectors Programme

importance_rationale: The reforms significantly impact the banking sector but do not constitute a major overhaul.
description: The Slovak government implemented several reforms in the banking sector, including an amendment to the Banking Act in January 1996 to enhance competitiveness, the establishment of the Agency for Industrial Development and Revitalisation (AIDR) in 1995 to provide consultancy services, and the creation of the Slovak Credit Institute (SUU) to support enterprise financing needs. Additionally, a Deposit Security Fund was established in March 1996 to guarantee deposits and enhance competition.
source_quote: This agency will provide consultancy services in technological development, foreign trade, marketing and banking to industries in the process of restructuring.

growth_orientation_rationale: The fund aims to promote local industries and infrastructure, which can enhance regional economic development and reduce disparities.
status_evidence: The Office for Strategy has been operational since 1995.
importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in regional policy management, aiming to address regional disparities through targeted funding and support.
description: The Slovak government established the Office for Strategy of the Development of the Society, Science and Technology, which is responsible for designing regional policy and overseeing the Regional Development Fund to support various regional development projects.
source_quote: Since 1995, regional development in Slovakia has been the responsibility of the Office for Strategy of the Development of the Society, Science and Technology of the Slovak Republic.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and training systems is expected to enhance the skills of the workforce, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully put into practice as part of the National Employment Plan and the National Programme for Upbringing and Education.

description: The Slovak Republic aims to enhance the educational system and improve the quality of education and training programs to address the lack of highly qualified human capital. This includes increasing resources for education, upgrading skills, and enhancing vocational training to boost employability and raise educational attainment.
source_quote: The government submitted to Parliament a programme in 2001 – The National Programme for Upbringing and Education – setting out a strategy for improving the quality of the education and training systems.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing private funding for higher education can enhance the quality and accessibility of education, leading to a more skilled workforce.
status_evidence: The law was adopted and is currently in force.

source_quote: The new Law on Universities, adopted in 2002, transforms tertiary institutions into non-governmental organisations, makes tuition fees for 'external' (part-time) students applicable...

growth_orientation_rationale: Improved fiscal planning and transparency can lead to better allocation of resources and more effective public investment.
status_evidence: The framework has been established and is currently in use.

description: In 2003, the Slovak Republic initiated a major reform to establish a medium-term budget framework, aiming to project government revenues and expenditures over a three-year period, thereby enhancing fiscal transparency and planning.
source_quote: The government then announced a major initiative in 2003, promising to submit a three-year budget framework to Parliament together with the 2005 budget act.

growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidies enhance the financial viability of starting new businesses, thereby promoting entrepreneurship and job creation.

description: Start-up subsidies were introduced in Slovakia in 2004 to encourage self-employment among job seekers, with conditions relaxed during the crisis to allow school graduates to apply immediately upon registering at the labor office.
source_quote: Start-up subsidies provided by the labour office increase the financial attractiveness of self-employment, at least in the first years.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing labour mobility can lead to a more efficient allocation of resources and boost overall productivity growth.

source_quote: Some steps have already been taken to improve labour mobility. Importantly, the transaction tax on house sales was simplified to a flat rate of 3% from January 2004 and eliminated completely as of January 2005.

growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing budget transparency and accountability, this reform aims to improve the efficiency of public spending, which can lead to better resource allocation and support economic growth.
status_evidence: The government has decided to give a new impulse to results-oriented budgeting.

description: Slovakia has been trying to introduce results-oriented budgeting since 2002 to subject public activities to explicit objectives, cost transparency, and performance tests, aiming to use the budget as a strategic resource allocation instrument.
source_quote: The introduction of results-oriented budgeting in which the budget process is centred around well-defined and measurable goals will play a crucial role.

growth_orientation_rationale: The feed-in tariffs provide financial incentives for the adoption of renewable energy technologies, which can lead to increased investment and innovation in the energy sector.

description: Slovakia introduced feed-in tariffs for electricity production from renewable energy sources (RES) to encourage investment in clean energy technologies. These tariffs guarantee a fixed price for electricity produced from RES for 15 years, promoting the development of biomass and solar energy production.
source_quote: Feed-in tariffs for electricity production from RES and from high-efficiency cogeneration were introduced in the electricity sector in January 2006.

source_quote: The Act on lifelong learning adopted in 2009 set a new regulatory framework for continuing education, for the accreditation and the assessment of training programmes.

growth_orientation_rationale: While the incentives aim to foster entrepreneurship, their lack of targeting and monitoring may lead to inefficiencies and limited impact on actual job creation.

description: Slovakia's start-up incentives consist of one-off subsidies for unemployed individuals starting a business, with spending on these measures increasing in recent years. However, the effectiveness of these programs is variable, with evidence suggesting low net positive impacts on employment due to high deadweight losses and lack of targeted monitoring.
source_quote: Start-up incentives consist in one-off subsidies allocated to the unemployed starting a business and are covered by two programmes in Slovakia.

growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to enhance access to R&D funding and support innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
package_name: R&D Tax Credit and Innovation Support
component_name: R&D Tax Credit and Innovation Vouchers
status_evidence: The R&D tax credit scheme was revised and implemented in 2013.
importance_rationale: The revisions to the R&D tax credit scheme and the introduction of innovation vouchers represent significant changes in the innovation support framework.
description: The Slovak authorities have revised the R&D tax credit scheme to make it more accessible for firms, particularly small and micro enterprises, by removing high eligibility thresholds. Additionally, they are considering widening the R&D tax credit for firms in less developed regions to hire researchers or engineers and make the tax credit refundable for firms that do not yet make profits. Furthermore, the government plans to develop innovation vouchers to promote knowledge diffusion, allowing firms to consult innovation service providers to identify and implement technological projects.
source_quote: The authorities could therefore give more consideration to widening the R&D tax credit for firms in less developed regions to hire researchers or engineers and make the tax credit refundable for firms which not yet make profits.

growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the efficiency of tax collection, the reform is expected to enhance public sector productivity and free up resources for other growth-enhancing initiatives.
status_evidence: The first stage of the UNITAS project is to be implemented by 2013.
importance_rationale: The UNITAS project represents a significant overhaul of the revenue collection system, aiming for substantial efficiency gains and improved public sector performance.
description: The Slovak authorities intend to increase the efficiency of the revenue system by moving to integrated revenue collection under the UNITAS project, which aims to merge tax and customs administrations and establish a new system for managing government revenues.
source_quote: The first stage, UNITASI, is to merge the existing tax and customs administrations and to establish a new system for the management and organisation of the collection of government revenues.

This includes exemptions from labor market tests for jobs in areas with low unemployment and a streamlined process for issuing work permits.
source_quote: Since May 2018 a facilitated procedure - including a labour market test exemption - is in place for occupations in shortage in districts where unemployment is below 5%.

source_quote: The 2013 National Reform Programme sets 2020 targets for gross domestic expenditure on R&D and business expenditure on research and development at 1.2% and 0.8% respectively.

They are also considering the development of short tertiary programmes that include on-the-job training and employable skills to better prepare students for the labor market and technological change.
source_quote: Developing short tertiary programmes, as currently considered by the authorities, that include on-the-job training and employable skills could provide the workers needed to use new technology.

source_quote: The new Research and Innovation Activities Act, which entered into force in January 2022, allows institutions to become co-owners of spin-offs.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the business environment and fostering entrepreneurship is expected to raise productivity and innovation.

source_quote: The quality of Slovenia’s future business environment depends, to a large extent, upon the introduction of an effective innovation policy, which could be supported by multi-purpose centres.

growth_orientation_rationale: By providing financial support to start-ups, the initiative aims to foster innovation and technological advancement, which can enhance productivity and economic growth.

source_quote: The government should reduce administrative dispersion and overlap among various stakeholders of innovation policy by improving information flows and transparency among ministries and associated agencies.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing educational attainment and job creation supports long-term economic growth by enhancing the skills of the workforce.

description: The Swedish government has significantly increased the number of places in the education system, adding over 90,000 new positions from 1991 to 1995 to enhance educational attainment and support the labor market. Additionally, the 1995/96 Budget proposal included measures worth SKr 7 billion to create jobs, notably through temporary recruitment subsidies.
source_quote: The 1995/96 Budget proposal also contained measures worth SKr 7 billion to create jobs, notably through temporary recruitment subsidies.

Additionally, financial aid is offered to unemployed persons starting their own enterprises, aiming to stimulate entrepreneurship and job creation in the private sector.
source_quote: The availability of seed capital was increased in 1992 by an injection of public funds of SKr 12 billion into two newly-established portfolio-investment companies and six new venture-capital companies.

growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing educational capacity and improving skills is expected to enhance human capital and productivity.
status_evidence: The reforms have been successfully implemented.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a major structural change in the education system.

source_quote: A substantial increase in the capacity of the formal education system, by 100,000 places in adult education aimed at low-skilled workers and 30,000 places in tertiary education.

growth_orientation_rationale: Improving education and labor market incentives is expected to enhance productivity and adaptability in the workforce.

importance_rationale: The reforms represent a structural change in the education system and labor market policies.
description: The Swedish government has implemented significant reforms in the education system, including the introduction of educational vouchers to enhance competition among schools and improve labor market incentives for both employers and employees. Additionally, there is a focus on enhancing adult and tertiary education to better align with labor market needs and consumer preferences.
source_quote: The government is relying heavily on education and training – specifically policies to promote adult and tertiary education – to eventually ensure the compatibility of present levels of labour costs with full employment.

growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating enterprise adaptability and promoting innovation among SMEs is expected to enhance economic growth and job creation.

description: The Swedish government has initiated a reassessment of the balance between employment protection and enterprise adaptability, alongside proposals to simplify the tax code and government support schemes for small and medium-sized enterprises, and measures to promote research and innovation among small firms through the 'Regional Development Programmes' and venture capital initiatives.
source_quote: Given a favourable overall cost level and a low corporate taxation, small and medium-sized enterprises may be best served by simplifying both the tax code and the wide array of government support schemes.

growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing technology diffusion and supporting innovation in SMEs is expected to raise productivity and economic growth.
status_evidence: The reform has been proposed but not yet implemented.

description: The Swedish government proposed a shift in the balance of support from university-based institutions to co-financed science parks, aiming to enhance technology diffusion to SMEs and stimulate the development of economically viable innovations in a business-oriented environment.
source_quote: Technology diffusion, arguably more important for enterprise and job creation than basic R&D, might benefit from transferring public R&D spending from universities to jointly funded science parks.

description: Sweden has introduced a system of differentiated fairway dues to provide economic incentives for shipping companies to reduce sulphur and nitrogen oxide emissions, encouraging the use of cleaner fuels and technologies.
source_quote: Sweden has introduced a system of differentiated fairway dues to provide economic incentives to reduce emissions from shipping.

growth_orientation_rationale: By addressing the skills gap and promoting personal investment in education, these initiatives are expected to enhance the workforce's capabilities, thereby supporting economic growth.

importance_rationale: Both initiatives represent significant reforms aimed at improving human capital in Sweden.
description: The Swedish government has implemented the Adult Education Initiative (AEI), which provides 100,000 full-time adult education places annually for unemployed individuals or those lacking full secondary qualifications. Additionally, a proposed guideline bill on individual learning and skills development aims to establish individual learning accounts to encourage personal investment in education and training.
source_quote: The Adult Education Initiative (AEI) is a major programme designed to redress the skills gap in Sweden and is aimed primarily at adults who are unemployed or who lack full three-year upper secondary qualifications.

growth_orientation_rationale: The subsidy is intended to stimulate construction activity, thereby increasing the housing supply and potentially improving economic conditions in the housing market.

source_quote: In spring 2003 Parliament passed legislation introducing a subsidy to new construction and rebuilding of small and medium-sized rental and student dwellings in the period 2003-06.

description: The Swedish government is developing measures to encourage entrepreneurship, including simplifying business regulations and providing grants to individuals wishing to start their own businesses.
source_quote: The government is developing or has already put in place a number of measures to encourage entrepreneurship.

growth_orientation_rationale: The system incentivizes renewable energy production, which can lead to innovation and investment in the sector.

source_quote: The electricity certificate system is the main instrument used by Sweden to develop renewables in the electricity sector.

growth_orientation_rationale: Removing the wealth tax can incentivize individuals to invest more in innovative ventures, thereby fostering economic growth.
status_evidence: From 2007, the wealth tax was abolished.
importance_rationale: The abolition of the wealth tax is a major reform that significantly alters the tax landscape, encouraging investment and innovation.
description: The government plans to abolish the wealth tax, which is expected to encourage wealth accumulation and investment in innovation.

growth_orientation_rationale: By incentivizing the purchase of household services, the reform encourages labor market participation and increases the availability of jobs, particularly for low-skilled workers.
status_evidence: The scheme has been in effect since July 2007.
importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant policy change aimed at improving labor market outcomes and supporting work-life balance, with potential positive effects on employment.
description: Since July 2007, Sweden introduced a tax deduction for household work, allowing each adult to claim a deduction worth half of what is paid for such services, up to a limit of 15% of average full-time earnings. This reform aims to facilitate the combination of work and family life while increasing job availability for low-skilled workers.
source_quote: Since July 2007, each adult can claim a tax deduction worth half of what is paid for household work in the person’s own or parents’ home up to a limit of 15% of average full-time earnings.

growth_orientation_rationale: Government funding for R&D is expected to stimulate innovation and economic growth by addressing under-investment in research due to market failures.
